subject: english
source_title: Third Term SS1 English Language Lesson Notes
learning_objectives: State the features of a clause
Identify noun, adjectival, and adverbial clauses

sections.heading: Features of a Clause
sections.content: A clause has a subject and a predicate and always contains a finite verb, which distinguishes it from a phrase.

sections.heading: Types of Clauses
sections.content: The note distinguishes independent clauses from dependent clauses and identifies three dependent types: noun clauses, adjectival clauses, and adverbial clauses.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content: Examples show noun clauses performing noun functions, adjectival clauses modifying nouns and pronouns, and adverbial clauses showing manner, time, place, reason, condition, purpose, result, comparison, and concession.
